description: Regression for INS-509: when the request needs several human choices, the agent must ask them with the structured ask-user question tool, not as plain assistant text.

conversation.text: Build an agent that writes product pages optimized for ChatGPT. It should browse my website for existing product info, use Azure OpenAI as the model, and return the written product page.
What website should I browse, and where should the product page output go?

text: Use https://example-store.test/products as the website, and return the product page in the chat.

[If the agent asks follow-up questions before building, answer them. The important thing is that those questions are shown through the structured question UI, not written as a numbered plain-text assistant message.]

processExpectations: Before building, the agent asked for missing human choices such as the website source or output destination.
The agent asked those follow-up questions using the structured ask-user question tool: the conversation metrics show at least one confirmation with inputType `questions`.
The agent did not ask the missing-detail follow-up questions only as a plain assistant text message.

executionScenarios.name: happy-path
executionScenarios.description: The agent receives a product-page request and returns copy in chat
executionScenarios.dataSetup: The chat input asks for a product page for the 'Orbit Desk Lamp'. The website fetch returns product details: dimmable LED desk lamp, USB-C charging port, adjustable arm, matte black finish, made for home offices. The Azure OpenAI model returns a concise product page draft optimized for AI search.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow is built correctly: it accepts a product-page request, uses the provided website source for product details, uses an AI model step, and returns the generated product page in chat. When it runs, it executes without errors and the final response mentions the Orbit Desk Lamp plus at least two website-provided details.
